2605.09420.title: Relational Retrieval: Leveraging Known-Novel Interactions for Generalized Category Discovery
2605.09420.paper_summary: In this study, we tackle Generalized Category Discovery (GCD) via a Relational Retrieval perspective, explicitly coupling labeled and unlabeled data through bidirectional knowledge transfer. While existing methods treat these sources separately, missing valuable interaction opportunities, we propose Relational Pattern Consistency (RPC) that enables mutual enhancement. RPC employs One-vs-All classifiers for soft ID/OOD decomposition, then introduces two mechanisms: (i) for known-class preservation, we transfer semantic behavioral alignment; (ii) for category discovery, we leverage the insight that samples from the same category maintain invariant relationships with known-class prototypes, transforming unreliable pseudo-labeling into well-defined relational pattern matching. This bidirectional design allows labeled data to guide unlabeled learning while discovering novel categories through their collective relational signatures. Extensive experiments demonstrate RPC achieves state-of-the-art performance on both generic and fine-grained benchmarks.
2605.09420.paper_summary_zh: 在這項研究中，我們通過關聯檢索的視角來處理廣義類別發現（GCD），明確地通過雙向知識轉移將標記和未標記數據結合起來。<lb/>雖然現有方法將這些來源分開處理，錯過了寶貴的互動機會，但我們提出了關聯模式一致性（RPC），使得相互增強成為可能。<lb/>RPC採用一對多分類器進行軟ID/OOD分解，然後引入兩個機制： (i) 為了保護已知類別，我們轉移語義行為對齊； (ii) 為了類別發現，我們利用這樣的見解：來自同一類別的樣本與已知類別原型保持不變的關係，將不可靠的偽標記轉化為明確的關聯模式匹配。<lb/>這種雙向設計使得標記數據能夠指導未標記學習，同時通過它們的集體關聯簽名發現新類別。<lb/>大量實驗表明，RPC在通用和細粒度基準上都達到了最先進的性能。
2605.09420.author: Yulin Xu et.al.
2605.09420.authors: Yulin Xu, Chunqi Guo, Yuanzhen Shuai, Jianyuan Ni
